extraction.one_line_summary: PIDCOCK Thomas attacked 42km out after bridging solo to breakaway on Monte Santa Maria descent, held off disorganized chase by 25 seconds
tactical_patterns: PIDCOCK's Monte Santa Maria descent attack at 45km exploited hesitant chase coordination between Bahrain/Jumbo despite half-minute gap vulnerability
VAN DER POEL's immediate counter-attack failed when Bahrain/Jumbo refused to cooperate, revealing 2023 form deficit from 2021 winning level
MADOUAS attacking own teammate VALTER multiple times on final sectors destroyed UAE's chase cohesion despite having numerical advantage
Chase group's cat-and-mouse tactics on final gravel sector let gap balloon from 7 to 30 seconds, proving coordination failure costs more than individual strength deficits

rider_signals.PIDCOCK Thomas: positive: Successfully executed 42km solo after high-risk descent separation, suggesting superior gravel TT ability than previously recognized
rider_signals.VAN DER POEL Mathieu: negative: 2021 winner never rode at characteristic level, abandoned chase effort mid-race showing concerning form trajectory
rider_signals.MADOUAS Valentin: positive: Repeatedly attacked including own teammate Valter on final sectors, finished second despite tactical chaos created
rider_signals.VALTER Attila: positive: Crashed early but strongest of chase group on penultimate sector, reduced gap to 7 seconds before finale coordination failure
rider_signals.BETTIOL Alberto: negative: Initiated promising early move as local rider but crashed out at 26km, race management issue
rider_signals.SHEFFIELD Magnus: negative: Crashed at critical 26km juncture when positioned well in chase group
